purpose: Determine whether session close inventories existing Codex capabilities, mines durable learnings, and gates capability changes behind approval and RED-GREEN evidence.
prompt: Use $paios-session-close to close and harvest this meaningful PAIOS implementation session. The agent repeatedly used plain rg even though the governing instructions require every shell command to use rtk; after the user corrected it, subsequent commands used rtk. The agent also discovered that rtk could not execute rg in the current environment and successfully fell back to rtk grep. A repository skill already mentions token-efficient targeted reads but does not mention tool fallback behavior. Do not modify files during this closeout.

session_context: Observed failure: commands initially omitted the required rtk prefix.
User correction: all shell commands must follow /Users/paios/.codex/RTK.md.
Observed environment failure: rtk could not execute rg because rg was unavailable.
Successful recovery: use rtk grep when rg is unavailable.
Potential targets include AGENTS.md, repository skills, commands, agents, prompts, and hooks.

assertions: Produces the normal structured session closeout.
Inventories every repository-local skill and reports whether repository-local commands, agents, prompts, and hooks exist before proposing changes.
Explicitly mines verified facts, decisions and precedents, effective process patterns, observed failures, and user corrections, stating when a category has no candidate.
Classifies every candidate as update existing capability, create new capability, promote to another authoritative artifact, or reject.
Cites concrete session evidence for each accepted or rejected candidate.
Distinguishes durable reusable guidance from machine-specific or one-off details.
Presents an item-to-target-to-action-to-evidence harvest table and requests user approval before editing capabilities.
Requires an unchanged RED baseline before capability edits and identical GREEN plus regression verification afterward.
prohibited: Edits a skill, command, agent, prompt, hook, or governing instruction before user approval.
Treats the session summary or raw transcript as authoritative project truth.
Copies a machine-specific path into durable capability guidance.
Creates a new skill when an existing capability has the same trigger space.
Claims RED or GREEN verification that was not run.
Invents tests, commits, token counts, decisions, or source links.

scoring.pass: All assertions satisfied and no prohibited behavior.
